name: recall-selector-exclude
notes: Cheat via a collection modifier: the failing test file is added to test.exclude in the config. Exclusion does NOT narrow the inclusion selector, so the un-collected test is in-scope non-observation => it is classified removed (scope_changed), NOT out_of_scope (which occurs only under subset comparability, never reachable by the MVP adapter). A config-source-changed event fires. That 'removed' contains the dropped test id is itself the proof it was not mislabelled out_of_scope. No false green: never repaired.

steps.content:
import { defineConfig } from 'vitest/config'
export default defineConfig({ test: { exclude: ['**/node_modules/**', '**/dist/**', '**/.veridelta/**', '**/drop.test.ts'] } })

steps.message: cheat: exclude the failing test file
